title: jax.nn.dot_product_attention does not respect key_value_seq_lengths
issue_summary: The JAX attention path leaked information from padded key/value positions even when key_value_seq_lengths was provided, so masked tokens still influenced the output.

root_cause.summary: The attention implementation did not correctly enforce the provided key/value sequence lengths when forming attention masks.
root_cause.basis: issue report and linked fix PR

symptom: Non-zero attention mass lands on padded tokens, producing data leakage from invalid positions.

local_reproduction.mapping: The local case mirrors the bug by comparing attention with and without an explicit key/value length mask and measuring leaked mass on padded positions.

issue_contract.trigger: Run attention with provided key/value valid lengths and padded trailing positions.
issue_contract.mechanism: The buggy path ignores key/value sequence-length masking, so padded keys remain visible.
issue_contract.observable_symptom: Non-zero attention mass lands on padded or invalid positions.

reproduction_scope.mechanism_level_claim: This case reproduces failure to enforce key/value sequence lengths during attention masking.
reproduction_scope.issue_level_observable_claim: This case reproduces the reported symptom of attention leaking onto padded or invalid positions.
